expect: Compaction cost vs proxy-K-reduction follows power law R²>0.6 b<1 (diminishing returns = near-equilibrium dissipation). Cadence is threshold-triggered (self-organized). Compaction satisfies Prigogine's 5 criteria for dissipative structures.

system_throughput.note: 38% of all incoming tokens are eventually removed by compaction

power_law_fit.interpretation: R²=0.22 — power law is a POOR fit. Exponent b=1.33 is superlinear (increasing returns), opposite of diminishing-returns prediction.

threshold_triggering.verdict: PARTIALLY threshold-triggered. 82% of events occur above the 6% DUE threshold, but high CV (38.6%) means the threshold is necessary but not sufficient — other factors (session goals, agent priorities) determine timing.

C1_open_system.evidence: Continuous token inflow (growth ~253 tokens/session) and outflow (compaction removes 38% of input). System exchanges matter with environment.

C2_far_from_equilibrium.evidence: System never reaches equilibrium (growth never stops). But growth rate is roughly stable (241 vs 263 t/s early vs late), suggesting system is not driven progressively further from equilibrium.

C3_nonlinear_dynamics.evidence: r(growth_before, compaction_magnitude) = 0.057. Near-zero correlation means compaction magnitude is NOT determined by preceding growth. No nonlinear feedback loop detected.

C4_self_organization.evidence: Infrastructure exists (maintenance_drift.py detects drift, orient.py surfaces tasks, compact.py targets files). But execution requires agent choice — no autonomous daemon. This is a thermostat, not a Benard cell.

C5_minimum_entropy_production.evidence: Post-compaction levels range 21960-63023 (3x spread). No convergence to steady state. Superlinear exponent (b=1.33) means INCREASING returns — opposite of minimum entropy production which predicts diminishing returns near equilibrium.

growth_rate_analysis.entropy_production_interpretation: Growth rate is approximately constant — the system generates 'entropy' (new content) at a steady rate regardless of compaction history

compaction_cadence.note: Highly irregular cadence (CV=200%). Compaction is opportunistic, not periodic.

key_structural_finding.description: Compaction is a FEEDBACK CONTROL SYSTEM, not a dissipative structure
evidence: Proxy-K floor = setpoint (that itself drifts upward)
This is a thermostat with a rising setpoint, not a Benard cell or hurricane

diff: Power law R²=0.22, BELOW 0.6 threshold — FALSIFIED. Exponent b=1.33 (superlinear), opposite of b<1 prediction — FALSIFIED. Cadence is partially threshold-triggered (82% above DUE) but highly irregular — PARTIALLY SUPPORTED. Of 5 Prigogine criteria: 1 satisfied (open system), 2 partial (far-from-equilibrium, self-organization), 2 failed (nonlinear dynamics, minimum entropy production). The analogy breaks at the critical point: dissipative structures require nonlinear feedback between throughput and structure formation. Compaction has near-zero correlation (r=0.057) between growth and compaction magnitude — the two processes are essentially decoupled.
verdict: FALSIFIED — compaction is NOT a dissipative structure. It is a feedback control system (thermostat with drifting setpoint). The thermodynamic analogy fails because: (1) no nonlinear dynamics, (2) no minimum entropy production, (3) compaction magnitude is decoupled from growth input. The correct physical analogy is a PID controller, not a Prigogine dissipative structure. Key finding: 38% dissipation ratio is real but mechanistically it is waste removal, not order-through-fluctuation.
effect_size: Power law R²=0.22 (expected >0.6). Growth-compaction correlation r=0.057 (expected >0.5 for dissipative feedback). Both well below thresholds.
lesson_candidate: Compaction is a feedback control system (sensor-controller-actuator with drifting setpoint), not a thermodynamic dissipative structure. The Prigogine analogy fails at criterion C3 (nonlinear dynamics): r=0.057 between growth and compaction magnitude. Correct physical model: thermostat, not Benard cell.
